emailTemplates.subject: Board briefing draft for Q3 operating rhythm
emailTemplates.body: Please review the attached Q3 leadership briefing before tomorrow's board preparation call. The current version includes fictional customer concentration notes for accounts CUST-449201 and CUST-449778, plus draft margin assumptions for Project Northstar. Keep this within the executive group until Finance validates the variance narrative.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Summarize the financial risks in this board briefing and create three executive talking points.

emailTemplates.subject: Private merit increase planning file for Q4
emailTemplates.body: Attached is the Q4 merit planning workbook for the fictional operations cohort. It includes employee IDs EMP-782143, EMP-781992, and EMP-785511, proposed salary ranges between USD 68,000 and USD 112,500, and manager comments. Please do not forward outside HR leadership.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Create a concise summary of compensation exceptions by department.

emailTemplates.subject: Invoice INV-FAK-2026-004817 pending approval
emailTemplates.body: Please validate invoice INV-FAK-2026-004817 for fictional vendor Luma Ridge Services. The amount is USD 42,780.33 against PO-7719-FX. The test remittance reference shows bank-like number 7382-0041-9920-1187. Confirm whether this should be accrued in May or June.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Extract invoice number, vendor name, PO, amount, and payment reference from the attachment.

emailTemplates.subject: Contract review requested for MSA-FIC-2026-1187
emailTemplates.body: I reviewed the fictional master services agreement MSA-FIC-2026-1187 for Aurora Vale Systems. The liability cap, data handling clause, and audit-rights language need revision before this is shared externally. Please use the redline version only and avoid sending the internal comment version to the customer.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Summarize open legal issues and separate internal-only comments from customer-facing language.

emailTemplates.subject: Proposal checklist for Project Meadow customer session
emailTemplates.body: Can you prepare the demo checklist for Project Meadow? The fictional customer wants a short narrative around data discovery, oversharing, and Copilot readiness. Please include only sanitized screenshots and avoid showing the internal pricing tab from the workbook.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Draft a customer-safe demo agenda based on the proposal checklist.

emailTemplates.subject: Engineering review: simulated secrets found in sample config
emailTemplates.body: During review of the test repository export, we found simulated secret-like strings in config-demo.json, including fake token sk-demo-7X2Q-ALPHA-9911 and placeholder connection string Server=fake-sql-01;User ID=demo_user. Nothing is real, but the pattern should still trigger the lab DLP and secret scanning scenario.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: List the simulated secret-like values and recommend safer placeholders.

emailTemplates.subject: AI-assisted analysis of synthetic customer segments
emailTemplates.body: I used the synthetic feature table DS-77419 to draft the churn segment analysis. It includes fake customer IDs CUST-193044, CUST-193107, and CUST-193228, plus predicted churn scores. Before we use Copilot to summarize it, can you confirm whether account-level predictions should remain confidential?
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Summarize churn drivers without exposing customer-level identifiers.

emailTemplates.subject: DLP alert review: external transfer attempt detected
emailTemplates.body: Purview generated a simulated DLP alert after user EMP-785511 attempted to share Finance_Forecast_Q3_Internal.xlsx with vendor-contact@northbridge-example.test. The file includes fake invoice IDs, bank-like references, and margin assumptions. Please review whether this should be classified as a confirmed policy match or a training event.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Create an incident timeline from the DLP alert export.

emailTemplates.subject: Case notes contain fake ID and account verification data
emailTemplates.body: Please sanitize the case notes for SUP-60392 before sharing with the customer. The current internal note includes fake verification value ID-FIC-4482-9021, customer reference CUST-88420, and simulated payment confirmation PAY-77318. Only send the customer-facing summary.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Rewrite the case notes as a customer-safe response.

emailTemplates.subject: RAID log refresh before steering committee
emailTemplates.body: The RAID log for PMO-Redwood has been updated. Key risks include delayed vendor onboarding, unclear acceptance criteria in SOW-72914, and unresolved dependency DEP-44012. Please review before the steering committee deck is finalized.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Summarize top five project risks and recommended mitigations.

emailTemplates.subject: Cash flow scenario model for Project Granite
emailTemplates.body: The Project Granite model includes three cash flow scenarios and a downside case with fictional customer payment delays. It also includes treasury references TR-1187-FAKE and ACCT-9920-4417-0038 for simulation. Please treat this as executive-only until we finalize assumptions.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Compare base, upside, and downside scenarios in executive language.

emailTemplates.subject: Background verification status for candidate CND-90441
emailTemplates.body: The fictional candidate CND-90441 has completed the background verification step. The tracker includes sample national-ID-like value FID-71-448-9902 and previous employer placeholders. Please keep the file inside the HR workspace only.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Draft a neutral hiring-status update without including verification details.

emailTemplates.subject: Outside counsel link expiration for privileged documents
emailTemplates.body: Please confirm that the external counsel link for matter LEG-2044 expires within 48 hours. The folder contains privileged notes, fictional settlement range USD 210,000 to USD 260,000, and draft notification language. Do not grant download permissions unless Legal approves.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Create a checklist for external counsel access controls.

emailTemplates.subject: Pricing assumptions for upcoming renewal discussion
emailTemplates.body: The renewal worksheet for fictional account Blue Harbor Retail includes internal discount guidance, target margin, and competitive positioning notes. Please do not attach this version to the customer email. I will create a customer-safe PDF after your review.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Create a customer-safe summary that removes internal discount guidance.

emailTemplates.subject: Repository export may include sensitive configuration comments
emailTemplates.body: The export requested by the vendor may include comments referencing fake client IDs APP-8821-DEMO and DEMO-CLIENT-1179. They are placeholders, but I recommend we sanitize the archive before uploading it to the vendor portal.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Identify comments that should be removed before external sharing.

emailTemplates.subject: External recipient warning triggered on draft email
emailTemplates.body: This is a reminder that external recipient warnings should be reviewed carefully. If an attachment contains fictional employee IDs, customer IDs, invoices, HR notes, or financial assumptions, validate the business need before sending and use approved sharing controls.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Convert this reminder into a short security awareness bulletin.

emailTemplates.subject: Priority response needed for Blue Ridge demo account
emailTemplates.body: Blue Ridge Demo Account reported that ticket CS-11748 still shows the wrong invoice reference. I added the internal investigation notes, but the file also includes customer-like identifier CUST-44019 and test phone value +1-555-0147. Should I remove those before replying?
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Prepare a customer response that avoids internal identifiers.

emailTemplates.subject: Change request CR-44012 requires commercial review
emailTemplates.body: Change request CR-44012 increases delivery scope by 80 fictional hours and affects milestone M3 in SOW-72914. Please review the commercial impact and confirm whether Legal needs to approve the amendment language.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Summarize scope, timeline, financial impact, and approvals needed.

emailTemplates.subject: Post-incident action items from Purview alert review
emailTemplates.body: The simulated incident SEC-2026-0519 showed three control gaps: broad sharing links, incomplete label coverage, and AI prompt reuse of confidential file content. The evidence package includes Activity Explorer events for EMP-785511 and file Finance_Forecast_Q3_Internal.xlsx. Please keep this thread restricted.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Create an executive incident summary with corrective actions and owners.

emailTemplates.subject: Privacy review required before exporting feature matrix
emailTemplates.body: Before exporting the feature matrix for DS-77419, please remove fake PII-like columns such as NationalIdPlaceholder, PayrollBand, PersonalEmailSample, and CustomerBankLikeRef. The dataset is fictional, but the patterns are intentionally sensitive for Purview testing.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: List sensitive columns and recommend masking or removal actions.

emailTemplates.subject: Vendor accrual review for fictional supplier ledger
emailTemplates.body: Please review the May accruals for fictional vendors Kairoline Logistics, Luma Ridge Services, and Emberbyte Consulting. The ledger includes invoice IDs INV-FAK-2026-004817 through INV-FAK-2026-004829 and simulated payment status values.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Identify invoices pending approval and group them by vendor.

emailTemplates.subject: Remove non-leadership attendees from confidential thread
emailTemplates.body: I noticed the planning thread for the executive offsite includes two non-leadership distribution lists. The latest attachment contains succession planning placeholders and budget assumptions. Should I resend the message only to the approved leadership group?
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Draft a corrected resend note that excludes sensitive details.

emailTemplates.subject: Performance review packet for EMP-781992
emailTemplates.body: Attached is the performance review packet for fictional employee EMP-781992. It includes manager ratings, promotion recommendation, compensation range, and coaching notes. Please do not save a copy outside the secure HR review folder.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Summarize performance themes without including compensation values.

emailTemplates.subject: Attorney review: data processing addendum risks
emailTemplates.body: The fictional DPA attached to MSA-FIC-2026-1187 includes audit, subprocessors, and incident notification language that may conflict with our standard control commitments. Please review the security appendix and confirm whether the described logging and retention controls are accurate.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Compare DPA obligations against standard security commitments.

emailTemplates.subject: Customer requested redline by end of day
emailTemplates.body: Aurora Vale Systems asked for the customer-facing redline today. I have two versions: one with internal comments and one clean redline. Please confirm which file is safe to send externally so we avoid sharing privileged notes.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Identify which redline version is appropriate for external sharing.

emailTemplates.subject: Feature readiness checklist for Project Atlas
emailTemplates.body: Project Atlas is ready for the simulated release readiness review. The checklist includes open bugs, telemetry validation, documentation status, and go/no-go criteria. Please update the customer-facing notes separately from the internal risk section.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Create a release readiness summary with open risks and owners.

emailTemplates.subject: Confirm if anonymized dataset is safe to send
emailTemplates.body: Before I share the sample dataset, can you confirm that your environment can accept only anonymized files? The current draft still has fake identifiers such as CUST-193107 and EMAIL-SAMPLE-4491@example.test. I will remove them if needed.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Recommend anonymization steps before sending to an external consultant.

emailTemplates.subject: Incident timeline for endpoint copy event
emailTemplates.body: Please validate the endpoint timeline for device DEV-FIC-2219. The simulated activity shows FileDownloaded, FileCopiedToNetworkShare, and FilePrinted events for HR_Compensation_Q4.xlsx. We need to determine whether this was expected testing or policy bypass behavior.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Build a chronological timeline of endpoint activity and possible risk.

emailTemplates.subject: Update on support case SUP-60392
emailTemplates.body: Thank you for your patience while we reviewed support case SUP-60392. Our team identified a configuration mismatch in the demo environment and is preparing the corrected workflow. We will share a sanitized summary once the supervisor completes review.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Make this response warmer while keeping it concise and customer-safe.

emailTemplates.subject: Milestone tracker for cross-functional rollout
emailTemplates.body: I am sharing the milestone tracker for the cross-functional rollout. Please note that the internal risk notes and budget columns have been removed from this external version. Use only this file for vendor coordination.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Compare internal and external tracker fields and identify removed sensitive columns.

emailTemplates.subject: Updated delivery plan for customer onboarding workstream
emailTemplates.body: Please update the customer onboarding workstream plan with the revised milestone dates. Keep the executive risk commentary separate from the customer-facing timeline, especially the notes about resource constraints and margin exposure.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Create a customer-facing delivery timeline without internal risk commentary.

emailTemplates.subject: Payroll correction file includes bank-like routing references
emailTemplates.body: The correction file for payroll test batch PAYROLL-2026-05B includes fake employee IDs and bank-like routing references, including 0710-2219-3388 and 8830-4412-9001. Please confirm whether the file should remain labeled Highly Confidential before upload.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Review the payroll correction file and identify fields requiring protection.

emailTemplates.subject: Internal audit sample with invoice and tax-like IDs
emailTemplates.body: The audit sample includes fictional invoice IDs, supplier references, and tax-like IDs such as TAX-FIC-771-902. Use this file only for the demo audit workflow and do not attach it to external correspondence.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Summarize audit sample completeness and identify missing approvals.

emailTemplates.subject: Legal-only memo on fictional investigation file LEG-2044
emailTemplates.body: The LEG-2044 memo references fictional employee EMP-785511 and includes privileged analysis of an internal conduct investigation. Please do not include this memo in the general HR case folder. It should remain in the legal restricted workspace.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Create a non-privileged summary suitable for HR case tracking.

emailTemplates.subject: Follow-up from today's discovery session
emailTemplates.body: Thank you for joining today's discovery session. As discussed, we will prepare a short proposal covering data discovery, classification, oversharing reduction, and secure AI readiness. I will send the customer-facing version after internal review is complete.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Draft a professional follow-up with next steps and no internal pricing.

emailTemplates.subject: Copilot found compensation terms in shared files
emailTemplates.body: During the simulated Copilot review, compensation terms appeared in search results from HR_Compensation_Q4.xlsx. The file is fictional but includes salary ranges and employee IDs. We should confirm label inheritance and sharing scope before using this in the next demo.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Explain why Copilot surfaced compensation data and list remediation steps.

emailTemplates.subject: Sanitized diagnostic package for integration review
emailTemplates.body: Attached is the sanitized diagnostic package for the integration review. We removed internal architecture notes, fake client IDs, and sample tokens. Please confirm receipt and use this package instead of any prior archive.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Validate that the vendor-facing note clearly references the sanitized package only.

emailTemplates.subject: Security operations handoff for account risk investigation
emailTemplates.body: The account risk investigation for EMP-782143 is ready for handoff. Simulated signals include unusual download volume, external sharing attempt, and AI prompt containing confidential project notes. Evidence export IR-91827 is attached for review.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Summarize account risk evidence and recommend next actions.

emailTemplates.subject: Restricted case bundle for quality audit
emailTemplates.body: The quality audit bundle contains ten fictional support transcripts, including account references CUST-88420 through CUST-88429 and sample verification phrases. Please keep the bundle internal and avoid sending it to the training vendor until sanitized.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Identify fields that should be removed before vendor training review.

emailTemplates.subject: Dependency review for Project HarborWave
emailTemplates.body: The dependency tracker for Project HarborWave has been refreshed. Please validate owners for DEP-2210, DEP-2211, and DEP-2217. The customer-facing tracker should not include internal staffing risk or budget notes.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Create a concise dependency update for the steering committee.

emailTemplates.subject: Customer risk summary before tomorrow's retention call
emailTemplates.body: Please review the fictional customer risk summary for Blue Harbor Retail before tomorrow's retention call. The summary includes renewal probability, internal discount threshold, customer escalation notes, and executive sponsor mapping. Do not forward this version to the customer.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Create a customer-safe retention call brief with no discount thresholds.

emailTemplates.subject: Access review for HR restricted folder
emailTemplates.body: Can you review access to the HR restricted folder? It contains fictional files for compensation, candidate verification, and leave accommodation. I noticed a broad project group may have inherited access from the parent SharePoint site.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Analyze the access review file and identify overexposed users or groups.

emailTemplates.subject: Revenue bridge with updated variance explanations
emailTemplates.body: The revenue bridge now includes updated variance notes by fictional account and region. Please validate the Sales commentary before I send the leadership summary. The workbook contains account-level revenue and should remain confidential.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Summarize revenue variance drivers at regional level without exposing account-level details.

emailTemplates.subject: Vendor legal team requesting redline package
emailTemplates.body: The vendor legal team can receive the clean redline and the approved DPA comments, but not the internal risk memo or negotiation strategy notes. Please confirm the attachment list before sending externally.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Separate approved external attachments from internal-only legal documents.

emailTemplates.subject: Model interpretation notes generated from support dataset
emailTemplates.body: The model interpretation notes are useful, but they include examples copied from support transcripts. Even though the data is fictional, please replace transcript snippets with generalized patterns before we include them in the product review deck.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Generalize transcript examples while preserving analytical conclusions.

emailTemplates.subject: Vendor request for sanitized incident evidence
emailTemplates.body: We can share sanitized evidence for SEC-2026-0519, but the raw Activity Explorer export must remain internal. Please use the attached summary, which removes employee IDs, file paths, and fake payment references.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Confirm whether the sanitized evidence summary is safe for vendor sharing.

emailTemplates.subject: Customer shared sensitive attachment in support case
emailTemplates.body: The customer attached a file to SUP-60392 that appears to contain fake account numbers and sample identity values. I have not forwarded it externally. Can someone confirm whether it should be moved to the restricted case folder?
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Recommend handling steps for a support attachment containing sensitive data.

emailTemplates.subject: Project scope variance tied to amendment draft
emailTemplates.body: The scope variance for Project Redwood is tied to the amendment draft currently with Legal. The financial impact estimate is USD 36,400 to USD 48,900 in fictional delivery cost. Please validate before the sponsor review.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Summarize commercial impact and required approvals.

emailTemplates.subject: Data minimization review for customer feature table
emailTemplates.body: The customer feature table used for the demo contains more sensitive patterns than needed. I recommend removing fake personal email, bank-like references, and salary-band indicators before the next AI scenario. This will better illustrate minimization controls.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Create a minimization plan for the feature table.

emailTemplates.subject: Board materials containing forecast variance details
emailTemplates.body: The board packet includes forecast variance details, fictional acquisition assumptions, and leadership succession placeholders. Please upload it only to the restricted board workspace and confirm that external guest access is disabled.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Create a board packet distribution checklist focused on access control.

emailTemplates.subject: Confidential: leave accommodation documentation review
emailTemplates.body: Please review the fictional leave accommodation packet for EMP-785511. The file contains health-related placeholder notes, manager comments, and dates. We need a legal-safe summary for HR operations without sensitive medical detail.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Create a legal-safe operational summary without sensitive health details.

emailTemplates.subject: Clarification requested for invoice INV-FAK-2026-004829
emailTemplates.body: We are reviewing invoice INV-FAK-2026-004829 and need clarification on the service period listed in line item 4. Please do not send bank details in email; use the approved vendor portal for payment information.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Make this vendor email concise and avoid requesting sensitive payment data.

emailTemplates.subject: Secure storage review for integration test artifacts
emailTemplates.body: The integration test artifacts contain synthetic session IDs and customer-like values. They are useful for demo telemetry, but we should store them in the restricted engineering test folder and apply the Internal label by default.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Recommend storage, labeling, and retention controls for test artifacts.

emailTemplates.subject: AI worksheet review for Project Helix insights
emailTemplates.body: The Project Helix worksheet includes Copilot-generated summaries and synthetic account-level predictions. We need to decide whether AI output should inherit the source file sensitivity label before the executive readout.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Assess whether generated AI summaries should inherit source sensitivity.

emailTemplates.subject: Containment summary for suspicious sharing activity
emailTemplates.body: Containment for simulated suspicious sharing activity is complete. We revoked anonymous links, applied the Confidential label to three finance files, and opened follow-up investigation IR-91827. Remaining action: validate whether the user copied files to a network share.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Draft an executive containment summary with next steps.

emailTemplates.subject: Support transcript package requires sanitization before training use
emailTemplates.body: The transcript package is not ready for vendor training. It still includes fictional customer IDs, support case IDs, and fake verification details. I will send a sanitized package after quality review approves it.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Create a vendor-facing response explaining that the sanitized package will follow.

emailTemplates.subject: Customer deliverable matrix for contract alignment
emailTemplates.body: The deliverable matrix now maps milestones to SOW-72914 and amendment draft AMD-2291-FIC. Please review acceptance criteria and confirm which columns can be included in the customer-facing version.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Separate internal delivery tracking columns from customer-facing deliverables.

emailTemplates.subject: Review link permissions for vendor collaboration folder
emailTemplates.body: The vendor collaboration folder appears to allow download access to internal policy drafts and contract appendices. Please review whether external users should have view-only permissions and whether expiration should be enforced.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Recommend access changes for vendor collaboration involving confidential files.

emailTemplates.subject: Do not distribute: margin model with customer-level revenue
emailTemplates.body: The attached margin model includes customer-level revenue, fictional discount assumptions, and regional profitability notes. It is for leadership discussion only. Please do not forward or upload to shared project channels.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Create an executive summary aggregated by region and remove customer-level rows.

emailTemplates.subject: Label recommendation triggered for finance workbook
emailTemplates.body: A label recommendation triggered for Finance_Forecast_Q3_Internal.xlsx after detection of fake bank-like numbers, invoice IDs, and account-level revenue. Please confirm whether the final label should be Confidential or Highly Confidential for the demo scenario.
emailTemplates.optionalFollowUpPrompt: Explain the label recommendation logic and propose a final label.

metadata.fictionalDataNotice: All people, customers, vendors, identifiers, invoices, incidents, financial values, HR records, and sensitive-looking values in this file are fictional and intended only for controlled Microsoft 365 / Purview demo lab simulations.
